summary: Post-bilateral mandibular fractures near the canines, genioglossus and masseter typically aid in jaw movement rather than retraction; thus other factors or specific muscles related to potential retractive forces need consideration. \boxed{} The summary highlights how genioglossus and masseter generally assist with opening/closing movements instead of causing retrograde displacement after injury, indicating an oversight regarding which exact muscles could lead to such effects based solely on provided information about their primary functions.
